business_description.nature_of_business: General contractor for commercial and institutional construction, including healthcare facilities, educational buildings, and corporate offices throughout Western Pennsylvania.

coverages_discussed.notes: Workers' Compensation coverage is requested for approximately 425 employees across all operations.
Requested on an occurrence form including standard premises/operations and products/completed operations.
Commercial Auto coverage requested for 15 vehicles including hired and non-owned auto.
Commercial Property coverage requested for all scheduled locations on a special form with replacement cost valuation.

document_info.prepared_by: Patricia Nowak, Senior Broker, Steel City Insurance Group

extracted_text_sections.claims_narrative: The claims history warrants review; management has implemented corrective measures to address identified exposures. Over the review period, the insured has incurred 52 total claims with 1315756.77 in total paid losses and 1489697.06 total incurred. Currently, 12 claims remain open and 40 have been closed. Annualized loss ratio is 18.7% (avg annual incurred 297939.41 against current annual premium 1589164.70 over 5 loss years). Breakdown by coverage line: Workers' Compensation: 35 claims, 1126509.19 paid, 1176100.41 incurred. General Liability: 8 claims, 105420.70 paid, 176946.61 incurred. Commercial Auto: 8 claims, 47076.38 paid, 99900.04 incurred. Commercial Property: 1 claim, 36750.00 paid, 36750.00 incurred.
extracted_text_sections.executive_summary: We are pleased to submit Keystone Commercial Builders, Inc. for your review and consideration. The insured is a general contractor for commercial and institutional construction — healthcare facilities, educational buildings, and corporate offices throughout Western Pennsylvania, headquartered in Pittsburgh, with approximately 42 years of operating history. Coverage is requested for Workers' Compensation, General Liability, Commercial Auto, Commercial Property.
extracted_text_sections.operations_description: The insured is a general contractor for commercial and institutional construction — healthcare facilities, educational buildings, and corporate offices throughout Western Pennsylvania, headquartered in Pittsburgh.
extracted_text_sections.safety_programs: The insured has implemented a formal return-to-work program that has reduced lost-time claims by over 30%. The insured maintains a dedicated safety committee that meets monthly and conducts quarterly site audits. Management has invested significantly in employee safety training, with all field personnel OSHA-certified. The insured maintains a formal business continuity and disaster recovery plan tested annually. The organization has achieved ISO 9001 certification. The insured carries contractual risk transfer provisions in all subcontractor and vendor agreements. Background checks and MVR reviews are conducted annually for all employees in safety-sensitive positions.

concerns: Claims history warrants review: 52 total claims over the review period, including 12 open claims.
Account experienced a cancellation/non-renewal in 2022-2023 with Zurich Insurance Group due to carrier book of business exit.
risk_highlights.loss_history_summary: Over the review period, the insured incurred 52 total claims with 1315756.77 in paid losses and 1489697.06 total incurred. Currently, 12 claims remain open and 40 are closed. Annualized loss ratio is 18.7%, based on average annual incurred of 297939.41 against current annual premium of 1589164.70 over 5 loss years.
positives: Formal return-to-work program reduced lost-time claims by over 30%.
Dedicated safety committee meets monthly and conducts quarterly site audits.
Significant employee safety training, with all field personnel OSHA-certified.
Formal business continuity and disaster recovery plan tested annually.
ISO 9001 certification reflects commitment to quality and process control.
Contractual risk transfer provisions are included in all subcontractor and vendor agreements.
Background checks and MVR reviews are conducted annually for employees in safety-sensitive positions.
425 employees and structured HR program support low turnover and consistent safety training.
42 years of continuous operations under consistent management demonstrates operational stability.
Broker characterizes the account as a quality account with strong operational controls.
